mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over consecutive 12-month windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of observations when UNRATE exceeds 4.5, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate collapsing consumer demand and credit stress, which suppresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline, with a 60-day forward return Sharpe ratio above 0.5 during co-declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of rising INDPRO, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 5.0 percent impose high financing costs on leveraged firms and compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity markets post positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of high-rate environments, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for inflation differentials.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust economic expansion, boosting corporate profit expectations and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than half of triggered periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth expands aggregate demand and corporate revenue bases, creating immediate positive price pressure on broad equity indices over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 110 index points signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces earnings momentum and triggers negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO falls below 110, or the predictive power of the threshold disappears after adjusting for sector rotation effects.
